item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. I know it's a bit necro but I'm really curious about the fact that the vast majority of people in and out of the town see him as alive.

Wishuns theory above is great. But if he is actually a corpse the only thing I can think of is that exposure to (or acceptance of) anomalies allows people to see him as a corpse. Which means Rhode is definately worth keeping an eye on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oooh, I like this theory. Would the memetic's effects remain intact whenever Mayor McMaster actually dies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think The Foundation has this anomaly backwards.
Let's compare…

People who see the mayor as a corpse:
-Foundation staff
-James Rhode, a twenty-something who makes life choices influenced by Jack Kerouac

People who see the mayor is just a guy:
-The 2,514 residents of Belleview
-The "vast majority" of other people

So which is more likely; that there is an anomaly that effects most people on the planet identically, causing whole populations to unwittingly collaborate in an elaborate farce passing off a corpse as an active politician, but that The Foundation (and one beatnik) are randomly immune to for no reason.

Or that there is a memetic agent giving a small group the shared illusion that Mayor McMaster is a mummy.

In this alternative scenario, Mr. Rhodes would be patient zero for the memetic.
No one in town caught the illusion from him because they already knew for a fact that the mayor wasn't a cadaver. Their personal experience acted like a mental vaccine. The few non-locals who heard Rhodes' story would dismiss it as lunacy, never having reason to encounter the mayor for the rest of their lives.
So Agent Matthews could easily have been the first person to A) hear the story and B) see the mayor for the first time, in that order.

After his report, every Foundation employee going to town would be briefed before arrival. Thus ensuring all staff are exposed to the memetic anomaly, and forcing The Foundation to come to the mistaken conclusions we read here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine this situation is the result of a deal with the devil worded "I want to be mayor forever", "I wish to have the town's most successful mayoral run ever" or a similar monkey's-paw scenario, love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my favorite SCP of yours. It's a good idea, but the way you carry it out and execute it for the audience is impressive, to say the least.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added, but with an adjustment of "connection" to "relation".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: edit made by Author

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to a skip with a similar theme of anomalous small town politics, I propose the following edit:

Additionally, such bills are usually phrased in such a way to benefit SCP-1679 in its own unique situation; many would be markedly less effective if enacted in neighbouring towns.

to

Additionally, such bills are usually phrased in such a way to benefit SCP-1679 in its own unique situation; many would be markedly less effective if enacted in neighbouring towns.1

Footnotes
1. Due to this, as well as other notable similarities, a possible connection to SCP-3088 is being investigated.

item.title: Something I just found
item.content:encoded:
This was originally inspired by the Wikipedia category "Politicians elected posthumously"…

… which includes https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_J._Rhoades.

Coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the SCP's concept enough to upvote it but I feel it could benefit from some polish. Some aspects of it are a bit awkward. For example:

After a short debriefing by Agent Mathews, Rhode himself was given C-class amnesic, and was transported to Donaldson Memorial Hospital with the cover story of being caught in an automobile accident.

Two things: first, "amnesic" is the term for a person with amnesia so the sentence implies that Rhode was offered a slave. The preferred terms would be "amnestic" or "amnesiac" (although "amnestic" sounds more genuinely scientific in my opinion).

Second, a car accident is a strange cover story for what happened to him and it should become very obvious very quickly to the hospital that it wasn't true. Why was he even taken to a hospital anyway when there's no indication of him suffering any injury?

One that would make more sense would be to tell him that he had a temporary mental breakdown that caused him to act recklessly and then forget all about it.

Of course, an even simpler solution would be to keep it to "he was given a class-C amnestic" and say no further. The cover story given, or the fact that one was given, is irrelevant (and I'm sure most people would assume it was done anyway so to specify it is redundant).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to this recently posted map, I live about 30min away from this SCP… Also, well done on the article, a very intriguing concept. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some of the paragraphs end in awkward clauses, such as "arrested before he could hurt anybody". Those should probably just be removed. Also, this may be a stylistic choice, but you have a "amnesic" in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of reminds me of Welcome to Night Vale, except in Night Vale everyone would be aware that he was dead, but wouldn't care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was actually coming here to post the same thing moose did. Honestly I think removing those sentences entirely would improve it. As it is, it just confused me as to what that actually meant.

"Bills that have allegedly been enacted by SCP-1679-1 have proven to be consistently and unusually effective in regards to improving the quality of life for its citizens."

That works fine by itself. Very good entry overall, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a joke about Congressmen and Senators here somewhere…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like a pretty nice town. I'd move there: at the end of the day, who cares if the mayor is alive or not?

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do ya mean he's dead? He's the best mayor that ever lived!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. That's a nitpick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weekend at Basil's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But whoever would try to assassinate good ol' Mayor McMaster? He's such a great leader!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. It seems so morbid, yet you feel like it should be left alone because of how resourceful he is. After all, it's not hurting anyone. I should have my grandfather pose for a photo suitable for this article.

Oh,

An Agent Matthews embedded in the largest nearby town's police force

Someone already pointed this out, but you didn't address them. Shouldn't this be "An agent (Hereby referred to as Agent Matthews) was embedded in the largest nearby town's police force"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my mind, yes, people do run against him. People vote for him, and he usually wins by a landslide.

His speeches consist of him sitting at a podium for 15 minutes in silence and suddenly the crowd bursts into thunderous applause.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im just seeing another corpse running against him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone ever run against him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to think that they'd just hold a funeral for him and find a new mayor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something really sublime about this. It's actually pretty simple, but very effective!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably [DATA EXPUNGED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am now reminded of the Lewis Black joke of how America should vote in a dead guy for president… preferably Reagan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Or more brain-dead ones," said Bob Humbug, generously setting up another commenter for a punchline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That would be ironic. Because as far i understand, this is the best politician ever.

Maybe we need more dead politicians.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. The concept, the hook, the containment procedures. I can't see anything to complain about. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As it turns out, all politicians are corpses and this is the only one that failed to convince everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure this guy beat John Ashcroft a few years back. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: in the town or not

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's also the fact that outsiders who've never seen it before also perceive it as a living being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd vote for him. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an anomaly because everyone, in the town or not, is going along with it, which is just feasibly impossible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, man. This seems like less of an anomaly than an Emperor Norton situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It wears a weathered grey suit with a red tie, and leans to the right of the wheelchair. SCP-1679-1 has never been witnessed moving, respiring, or making vocalizations in public or in private.

I think that's why he is at a slight angle in front of the camera-so he is facing it head on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Is this the final product of the raisin-Senator idea? One quibble:

During this block, SCP-1679-1 will be situated at a slight angle, so as to be facing the camera (and thus the viewer), for the full quarter hour.

This honestly doesn't make sense to me, and additionally seems surplus to requirements. Perhaps instead we could have a line clarifying that -1 doesn't actually move or do anything during the slot:

During this block, SCP-1679-1 will be positioned in front of the camera as though for a public address for the full quarter hour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Digging the concept. Tone could use a bit of polish but in all it's a solid piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept, writing, pace and tone are all great. I also kept chuckling at the idea itself, which seems to be a tongue-in-cheek reference to several elected officials who managed to beat an opponent from beyond the grave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idea: McMasters was the reverend of the local Fifthist church. Something something something creepy dead mayor. Thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could try:

"Additionally, such bills could only have been designed with an intricate knowledge of the town (its resources, political desires, etc)."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right! He was already dead when they elected him.

And regarding the second point… a couple things could happen. What do YOU think would happen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, wait, wait. He was not elected the first time until he died, was he? And no one seems to notice/care he's dead. Impressive work. You may have my up ore. And wear it proudly.

Question: what if they attempt to assassinate him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm out for dead presidents mayors to represent me.

Also,

An Agent Matthews imbedded

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking something about corn.

Or trans fat bans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I've been telling people we should do: elect the dead guy who screwed things up the least. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have less than no idea of how to draft and customize a legal bill to fit the situation of a fictional town with no set location.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good.

One nitpick:

Additionally, such bills are usually phrased in such a way to benefit SCP-1679 in its own unique situation; many would be markedly less effective if enacted in neighbouring towns.

Seems like it could use more elaboration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ya gotta stick with what works, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is why they keep electing dead politicians?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you to Voct for the idea. Thank you to Troy for getting my ass in gear. Thank you to Echo and Scantron for reviews. Echo also gave me the list name, which is fantastic.
